name: Urban League Of Broward County
description_short: Non-profit community organization providing free education, job training, housing counseling, and financial literacy services to Broward County residents.
description_long:
Urban League of Broward County is a longstanding community development organization celebrating its 50th anniversary, operating as a non-profit agent for change across South Florida. The organization serves as a comprehensive resource hub addressing systemic inequities through multiple service lines focused on economic empowerment, education, and community development.

The organization offers a broad portfolio of free and subsidized services including youth education and tutoring, college preparation, job training and placement through their Center for Working Families, business entrepreneurship support via their M3 Model (Money, Market, & Management), housing counseling with foreclosure prevention and loan modification assistance, financial literacy education, budgeting support, and health and wellness programs. They also operate justice-focused youth programs using restorative justice models and community engagement initiatives.

Urban League of Broward County distinguishes itself through integrated service delivery combining education, economic development, and housing support under one organization rather than fragmented providers. Their entrepreneurship center specifically helps small business owners access lending solutions and technical assistance, while their housing programs emphasize wealth-building through homeownership and asset accumulation rather than temporary relief.

As a non-profit entity affiliated with the national Urban League network, they operate transparently with board oversight and community accountability. However, specific outcome metrics are not populated on their website, making it difficult to independently verify impact claims. Service availability and eligibility requirements are not detailed online, requiring direct contact for program specifics.
pros: Comprehensive integrated services spanning education, jobs, housing, entrepreneurship, and health in one organization
Free financial literacy education, budgeting assistance, and credit counseling for low-income residents
Housing counseling includes foreclosure prevention and loan modification—practical debt relief alternatives
Jobs & Training center offers actual job placement services alongside training, not just referrals
Entrepreneurship Center provides technical assistance and lending solutions, not just workshops
Non-profit structure with 50-year track record and national Urban League affiliation
Serves as HUD-approved housing counselor resource for homebuyer education
cons: Website shows 0 for all impact metrics (served annually, entrepreneurs connected, homebuyers trained), making outcomes unverifiable
No pricing, eligibility criteria, or service access information published online
Limited details on specific program capacity, waitlists, or service availability
No online intake or application process visible; requires phone/email contact for enrollment
Geographically limited to Broward County despite serving broader South Florida areas
best_for: Broward County residents seeking integrated support combining job training with financial literacy and housing counseling
Low-to-moderate income individuals and families building toward homeownership or facing foreclosure
Small business owners needing technical assistance, business training, and lending access
Young people and families in Broward seeking affordable education support, tutoring, and college preparation
diagnosis: Urban League of Broward County is best for low-to-moderate income Broward County residents needing comprehensive, integrated support across education, job training, financial management, and housing—particularly those working toward homeownership or facing foreclosure. The main caveat is that specific program details, eligibility requirements, and current capacity are not available online, requiring direct contact to determine if services match individual circumstances and current availability.
services: Free credit counseling and financial literacy education
Housing counseling including foreclosure prevention and loan modification
Homebuyer education and financial preparation for home purchase
Job training and career placement services
Center for Working Families economic resource programs
Entrepreneurship Center with M3 Model (Money, Market, Management) business support
Business management training and technical assistance for small business owners
Small business lending solutions and access
Youth tutoring and academic advancement programs
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
